lastEpochBeginnerGuide.title: Last Epoch Beginner Guide
lastEpochBeginnerGuide.intro: Last Epoch rewards players who make a few good decisions early: pick a playstyle you can sustain, lock in your mastery on time, keep your gear updated with simple crafting, and move into Monoliths with a focused setup instead of scattered upgrades.

steps.title: Choose a class with a clear first-character identity
steps.description: Last Epoch starts with 5 base classes and 15 total masteries. For a smooth first run, Sentinel into Void Knight is the safest all-round recommendation, while Sorcerer and Paladin are also strong beginner-friendly picks.
highlights: Sentinel: tanky melee foundation with armor, shields, and strong survivability
Mage: ranged spellcasting with fast screen clear and strong AoE options
Acolyte, Primalist, and Rogue all have viable first-character routes once you know the fantasy you want

steps.title: Follow the campaign instead of over-farming early zones
steps.description: On a first playthrough, the story route is the best backbone because it naturally feeds skill points, idol slots, and key progression rewards. Keep moving forward instead of trying to perfect gear too early.
highlights: Prioritize the main story and side quests that reward passive points or idol slots
Avoid Hardcore for a first run unless you already know the game well
Do not lock yourself into solo challenge settings if you want shared stash, gold, or grouping flexibility

steps.title: Unlock your mastery as soon as Chapter 4 allows it
steps.description: Your mastery choice defines the character. To unlock it, spend 20 points in your base class passive tree, complete the Chapter 4 mastery quest, then speak with Elder Gaspar at the End of Time.
highlights: Mastery is permanent for that character
Skill and passive respecs are flexible, but mastery choice is not
Choose based on long-term playstyle, not only on the first few levels

steps.title: Build around 5 linked skills and one damage direction
steps.description: The fastest, cleanest characters use five specialized skills that reinforce each other and scale the same damage family. Mixing unrelated damage types slows down leveling and makes gear upgrades weaker.
highlights: Pick one main damage focus such as Void, Fire, Necrotic, Physical, or Bleed
Use your skill trees to make one core skill stronger instead of spreading power everywhere
Respec one active skill at a time so the rest of the build keeps carrying your XP and damage

steps.title: Start crafting early and keep your weapon current
steps.description: Crafting is one of Last Epoch's best systems and it matters during the campaign, not only at endgame. Use simple upgrades to keep damage and defenses on curve.
highlights: Buy Rune of Shattering whenever you can and break down useful affixes into shards
Use Glyphs of Hope and craft on items with good Forging Potential
Upgrade your weapon regularly because it is your main source of damage

steps.title: Prepare for endgame before the difficulty spike
steps.description: Before serious endgame pushing, get your character into a stable state. Around level 45 to 50, finish key campaign rewards, tighten your passives, unlock idol slots, and move into Monolith of Fate with a focused setup.
highlights: Endgame modes are accessed from the End of Time hub
Monolith of Fate is the main progression target once the campaign is done
Empowered Monolith becomes the long-term goal as your build stabilizes

quickTips: Sentinel into Void Knight is the safest first-character mastery path for new players.
Always complete passive-point and idol-slot side quests before moving to endgame.
Movement speed is a real power stat during leveling: prioritize it on boots and rings.
Craft early and often instead of waiting for perfect drops to fall.

lastEpochBestBuildsTierList.title: Last Epoch Best Builds Tier List
lastEpochBestBuildsTierList.intro: In Last Epoch, the best build depends on the job. Some builds dominate high-corruption Monolith pushing, some are built to delete bosses, and others win by clearing echoes faster than everything else. This module gives you a clean at-a-glance shortlist of the current high-value archetypes.

builds.name: Falconer Bomb Lance
builds.summary: One of the strongest high-corruption pushers. Combines Falcon scaling, Rogue mobility, and powerful imprint crafting upside.
highlights: Excellent corruption scaling
Fast movement and strong defensive layers
Extremely high damage ceiling

builds.name: Curse Warlock
builds.summary: Stands out through multiplicative curse scaling and strong damage-over-time pressure. Rewards players who want a modern caster with real endgame depth.
highlights: Very strong curse stacking
High DoT pressure in long fights
Scales hard into deeper empowered timelines

builds.name: Elemental Runemaster
builds.summary: Delivers huge elemental burst and strong battlefield control once the rune system clicks. One of the best ceiling builds for high-output casters.
highlights: Massive burst windows
Strong defensive passive options
Excellent scaling with proper rune management

builds.name: Echo Void Knight
builds.summary: A top boss killer built around reliable single-target Void damage and Sentinel durability. One of the cleanest ways to learn multi-phase bosses.
highlights: Strong single-target output
Reliable damage uptime
Sentinel defenses make boss progression smoother

builds.name: Reaper Form Lich
builds.summary: Trades safety for an elite bossing ceiling. With sustain handled correctly, Reaper Form turns the class into one of the fastest dedicated boss killers.
highlights: Very high boss damage ceiling
Life leech sustain enables aggressive uptime
Best for players comfortable with health-drain mechanics

builds.name: Holy Aura Paladin
builds.summary: The most forgiving bossing specialist for progression-focused players. Holy Aura and Healing Hands interactions give it exceptional staying power.
highlights: Excellent self-sustain
Very safe progression choice
Great first serious bossing character

builds.name: Shadow Cascade Bladedancer
builds.summary: The gold standard for echo clear speed. Shadow-heavy setups move through regular farming content at a pace that very few other masteries can match.
highlights: Best-in-class movement and flow
Exceptional echo clear speed
Ideal for loot-per-hour focused farming

builds.name: Hail of Arrows Marksman
builds.summary: Broad screen coverage with less mechanical intensity than Bladedancer. One of the best ranged farming choices for cleaner positioning.
highlights: Large AoE coverage per cast
Strong ranged farming profile
Easier to pilot than melee speed setups

builds.name: Storm Totem Shaman
builds.summary: A strong farming alternative for players who prefer totems and elemental storm setups. Tornado and Storm Totem keep damage rolling while the player moves.
highlights: Persistent damage while repositioning
Good map flow for a Primalist archetype
Strong option for players who dislike glass-cannon builds

lastEpochMasteryGuide.title: Last Epoch Mastery Guide
lastEpochMasteryGuide.intro: Mastery is the most important character commitment in Last Epoch. Every base class branches into three masteries, each with its own exclusive skills, play pattern, and endgame identity. Compare the whole board before speaking to Elder Gaspar.

masteries.name: Forge Guard
masteries.summary: A durable mastery that leans into armor, defense, and steady weapon-based damage.

masteries.name: Paladin
masteries.summary: A forgiving mastery with excellent sustain and one of the best progression profiles for new players.

masteries.name: Void Knight
masteries.summary: Built around Echo mechanics that duplicate key actions and add free damage to a simple melee loop.

masteries.name: Lich
masteries.summary: A high-ceiling mastery that converts health management into explosive damage and strong bossing.

masteries.name: Necromancer
masteries.summary: The classic summoner route, built around large minion armies and easy-to-read scaling.

masteries.name: Warlock
masteries.summary: A corruption-focused mastery that weakens enemies, stacks curses, and scales extremely well into endgame.

masteries.name: Sorcerer
masteries.summary: One of the easiest caster entries for new players thanks to clear AoE patterns and straightforward damage scaling.

masteries.name: Runemaster
masteries.summary: A high-skill, high-reward mastery that turns rune sequencing into one of the most powerful caster packages in the game.

masteries.name: Spellblade
masteries.summary: A close-range elemental fighter that mixes weapon hits with spell procs for a more active rhythm.

masteries.name: Druid
masteries.summary: A flexible mastery that shifts between forms and can cover tanking, healing, and sustained melee pressure.

masteries.name: Beastmaster
masteries.summary: Focused on permanent companions and beast synergy instead of temporary swarms or spell totems.

masteries.name: Shaman
masteries.summary: A stationary-control mastery that wins through repeated elemental setups and persistent damage sources.

masteries.name: Bladedancer
masteries.summary: The highest-tempo melee Rogue mastery, built around speed, dodge, and explosive clear patterns.

masteries.name: Marksman
masteries.summary: A clean ranged mastery for players who want safer positioning and broad screen coverage.

masteries.name: Falconer
masteries.summary: A top-tier Rogue mastery that mixes setup tools, companion pressure, and excellent corruption performance.

lastEpochLevelingGuide.title: Last Epoch Leveling Guide
lastEpochLevelingGuide.intro: Leveling in Last Epoch is two progress bars at once: your character level and your specialized skill levels. The best routes keep both moving together, use movement speed as a real power stat, and pivot into Monoliths with the right blessing and timeline order.

steps.title: Build your campaign around one damage family
steps.description: Your 5 specialized skills should support each other. Gear works best when your main skills all scale from the same damage profile instead of splitting between unrelated elements.
highlights: Character level caps at 100
Specialized skills normally cap at 20
Do not spread your active setup across different damage types while leveling

steps.title: Take the story route and collect permanent rewards
steps.description: Main story progression and side quests that grant passive points or idol slots should be handled early. These permanent rewards make every later leveling method more efficient.
highlights: Quests, enemy kills, Monoliths, Arena, and Echoes all contribute XP
Complete passive-point and idol-slot quests before serious Monolith farming
Track missing permanent rewards from the map interface

steps.title: Lock in your mastery when Chapter 4 opens it
steps.description: Spend 20 points in the base class tree, complete the mastery quest, then choose your subclass at the End of Time. Start spending into mastery scaling instead of delaying the power spike.
highlights: Mastery selection happens through Elder Gaspar
Choosing mastery early keeps your leveling build on curve
Respec passives and skills when needed, but keep the core identity stable

steps.title: Use movement speed as a leveling stat
steps.description: Movement speed is one of the strongest campaign accelerators because it improves kill rate, zone completion speed, and access to later progression systems.
highlights: Silver Ring gives 3 to 8 percent movement speed per ring
Advent of the Erased Boots can roll up to 20 percent movement speed
Lessons of the Metropolis Boots can roll up to 17 percent movement speed

steps.title: Craft often and keep replacing weak weapons
steps.description: Weapon damage and simple crafted affixes do more for leveling speed than waiting for perfect drops. The campaign gets much smoother when you treat the crafting bench as part of the normal route.
highlights: Use Rune of Shattering to stock shards for future upgrades
Use Glyphs of Hope to protect item durability while crafting
Update your main weapon after major zones instead of only after bad dry streaks

steps.title: Use Monolith Arena Nodes and the first XP blessing
steps.description: Once Monoliths open through the story, Arena Nodes are one of the best XP bursts in the game. They stay efficient until roughly level 58, then the route shifts toward the first key blessing.
highlights: Rare and magical enemies grant 50 percent more XP than normal enemies
Arena Node farming stays strong to about level 58
Defeating the first Monolith boss grants Strength of Mind, an experience blessing

steps.title: Unlock Empowered Monoliths around level 90
steps.description: Empowered Monoliths are the real long-form endgame engine. Clear the required timeline sequence, then return to efficient farming with higher reward scaling.
highlights: Complete Fall of the Outcasts, Black Sun, End of the Storm, and Reign of Dragons
Then clear Spirits of Fire, The Last Ruin, and Age of Winter
After unlocking Empowered Monoliths, farm Fall of the Outcasts again for Grand Strength of Mind

quickTips: Keep your weapon updated every few zones to maintain smooth kill speed.
Movement speed on boots and rings accelerates campaign progression significantly.
Arena Nodes in Monolith are one of the strongest XP sources before level 58.
Unlock Empowered Monoliths by completing the required timeline sequence starting around level 90.

lastEpochEndgameGuide.title: Last Epoch Endgame Guide
lastEpochEndgameGuide.intro: Last Epoch's endgame is built around repeatable systems that reward permanent power, targeted farming, and harder progression loops. Understanding which activity gives progression, loot, or build-pushing potential helps you plan your time after the campaign.

cards.name: Monolith of Fate
cards.description: The main endgame system. Players enter from the End of Time, clear echoes and quest objectives inside timelines, defeat the timeline boss, and earn permanent Blessings plus boss-specific unique drops.
highlights: The first Monolith zone starts at Level 58
Each timeline has its own quests, echoes, boss, and reward pool
Blessings permanently improve character power or loot quality

cards.name: Empowered Monolith
cards.description: Empowered Monoliths are the true long-term farming loop. They start at a much higher challenge level, let players push corruption far beyond normal timelines, and upgrade normal Blessings into Grand Blessings.
highlights: Unlocked after clearing The Age of Winter, The Last Ruin, and The Spirits of Fire
Runs at Level 100
Supports infinite corruption farming and stronger Grand Blessings

cards.name: Dungeons
cards.description: Dungeons are key-gated endgame runs with their own mechanics and reward identities. Each dungeon solves a different loot or crafting goal.
highlights: Lightless Arbor opens the Vault for gold-based chest rewards
Soulfire Bastion unlocks the Soul Gambler for rare and unique loot
Temporal Sanctum unlocks Legendary crafting through the Eternity Cache

cards.name: Arena
cards.description: Arena is the endurance mode of Last Epoch. It lets players test survival, scaling damage, and consistency across long combat runs rather than short boss bursts.
highlights: Arena of Champions ends after 40 waves and a boss
Endless Arena scales until the player retreats or dies
Runs can be compared on the leaderboard

cards.name: The Woven and Woven Echoes
cards.description: Season 2 expanded the Monolith with Tombs, Cemeteries, Memory Amber, Woven Echoes, and the Weaver Tree. This system adds another layer of endgame routing by letting players reshape encounters and rewards inside the Monolith.
highlights: Memory Amber is earned in Tombs and Cemeteries
The Weaver Tree grants up to 50 points
Roughly 70 Weaver Tree nodes modify Monolith rewards and encounters

cards.name: Season 4 Omens and Echo Chains
cards.description: Season 4 added Omen Windows, Echo Chains, Timeglass Fragments, Runes of Corruption, Omen Idols, and Idol Altars. These systems tie combat, Monolith routing, idol optimization, and high-risk item crafting together.
highlights: Five Echo Chain types launched with Season 4
Omens can drop Timeglass Fragments, Runes of Corruption, Corrupted items, Omen Idols, and Idol Altars
Timeglass Fragments can be spent at the center of the Monolith

lastEpochMonolithGuide.title: Last Epoch Monolith of Fate Guide
lastEpochMonolithGuide.intro: The Monolith of Fate is the backbone of Last Epoch's endgame progression. This guide walks you from the first Level 58 timeline to Empowered Monolith farming, boss-targeting, and corruption pushing.

steps.title: Enter the Monolith from the End of Time
steps.description: The Monolith of Fate becomes available after the campaign. Your first timeline begins at Level 58, so this is the point where your character transitions from story progress into repeatable endgame farming.

steps.title: Clear Echoes and Build Timeline Stability
steps.description: Each timeline is made of echoes with random objectives and modifiers. Completing them builds Timeline Stability, reveals more of the web, and pushes you toward the timeline's quest echoes.

steps.title: Finish the Timeline Quests and Kill the Boss
steps.description: Every timeline contains three quest stages that lead into the timeline boss. Beating the boss is what converts your run into permanent account progress through Blessings and boss-specific loot access.

steps.title: Take a Blessing and Move to Higher Timelines
steps.description: After each boss kill, choose a Blessing. The general progression advice is to keep moving right across the Monolith islands toward higher-level timelines while collecting the Blessings and boss drops that help your build most.

steps.title: Unlock Empowered Monoliths
steps.description: Clear the normal versions of The Age of Winter, The Last Ruin, and The Spirits of Fire to unlock the Empowered Monolith. Empowered timelines run at Level 100, start with much higher corruption, and replace normal Blessings with stronger Grand Blessings.

steps.title: Push Corruption with Shade of Orobyss
steps.description: Corruption is the main risk-and-reward lever inside the Monolith. Higher corruption makes enemies and echoes harder, but improves experience and item quality, turning the Shade of Orobyss into a key fight for long-term farming.

steps.title: Use Special Echoes, Woven Systems, and Echo Chains
steps.description: Modern Monolith routing includes Beacon Echoes, Vessel Echoes, Shade triggers, Woven Echoes, Tombs and Cemeteries, and now Echo Chains and Omen Windows. These layers let players target stability, encounters, idols, corruption tools, and specialized rewards more efficiently.

quickTips: Start moving right across the Monolith islands to unlock higher-level timelines quickly.
Always pick the Blessing that benefits your main build before farming other timelines.
Empowered Monoliths unlock after clearing three specific normal timelines.
Use the Shade of Orobyss to push corruption for better drops and XP scaling.

lastEpochCraftingGuide.title: Last Epoch Crafting Guide
lastEpochCraftingGuide.intro: Crafting is one of Last Epoch's defining systems, and it matters from the first upgrade all the way to high-end legendary and corruption crafting. This guide shows how the Forge works, why Forging Potential matters, and how crafting evolves into endgame progression.

steps.title: Learn the Forge Basics
steps.description: Press F to open the Forge anywhere. Most craftable items use up to two Prefixes and two Suffixes, while Sealed Affixes can add one more locked affix slot on top of the normal four-affix structure.

steps.title: Manage Forging Potential
steps.description: Forging Potential is the durability budget of an item. Common through Rare items usually have around 20 to 40 FP, while Exalted items often have 40 or more, so good crafting is really about spending that budget on the most important upgrades first.

steps.title: Use Shards for Core Upgrades
steps.description: Affix Shards are the base currency of crafting. They add or upgrade stats such as health, resistance, damage, attributes, or minion modifiers, and they are the foundation of nearly every gear improvement path in the game.

steps.title: Know the Important Glyphs
steps.description: Glyph of Hope gives a 25 percent chance to avoid FP cost entirely. Glyph of Order preserves value rolls when raising tiers, Glyph of Chaos rerolls an affix into another affix, Glyph of Despair seals an affix, and Glyph of Envy changes the rest of the item while leaving the upgraded affix alone.

steps.title: Know the Important Runes
steps.description: Rune of Shattering turns gear into shards, Rune of Removal deletes a random affix, Rune of Discovery fills an empty slot, Rune of Refinement rerolls affix values, Rune of Shaping rerolls implicits, Rune of Ascendance upgrades a normal item into a random Unique or Set item of the same base type, and Rune of Creation duplicates an item and sets both copies to zero FP.

steps.title: Chase Exalted and Legendary Crafting
steps.description: Exalted items are drops with at least one Tier 6 or Tier 7 affix. Legendary crafting then combines a Unique with Legendary Potential and an Exalted item in the Eternity Cache inside Temporal Sanctum, with LP1 to LP4 controlling how many affixes can transfer.

steps.title: Use the Eternity Cache Correctly
steps.description: Temporal Sanctum controls the level cap for Legendary crafting: Tier 1 supports Uniques up to Level 50, Tier 2 up to Level 65, Tier 3 up to Level 75, and Tier 4 removes the level restriction. Tier 2 and above also let players lock in one chosen affix during the slam.

steps.title: Expand into Modern Endgame Crafting Systems
steps.description: Season 2 added Woven Echoes, Woven Enchanting for class idols, Set bonus affix crafting, and Rune of Redemption support for fixing Exalted items. Season 4 then added Runes of Corruption and corrupted items, creating a new risk-heavy layer on top of the normal Forge.

quickTips: Always craft the most important affix first while Forging Potential is still high.
Glyph of Hope is your best friend for budget-conscious crafting during leveling.
Save Exalted items with good affix combinations for legendary crafting later.
Rune of Shattering on unwanted gear is the fastest way to stock up on crafting shards.

lastEpochLootFilterGuide.title: Last Epoch Loot Filter Guide
lastEpochLootFilterGuide.intro: Loot filters are one of the highest-value quality-of-life systems in Last Epoch because they turn clutter into readable progression. This guide helps you understand what to show, what to hide, and when to make the filter stricter.

steps.title: Understand What a Loot Filter Actually Does
steps.description: Loot filters highlight the affixes and item types you care about, recolor valuable drops, and hide low-value clutter. In Last Epoch, the system can highlight one wanted affix or groups of many affixes, making ground loot readable even deep into endgame.

steps.title: Start from a Shared Filter or a Simple Template
steps.description: A good first move is to begin with a general filter or a build filter from Last Epoch Tools, then tune it for your class and goals. This is faster than building from scratch and gives newer players a working structure immediately.

steps.title: Import a Filter into the Game
steps.description: Last Epoch Tools supports export and clipboard copy for filters. The normal workflow is to open the filter page, press Export, copy it to the clipboard, then paste it into the in-game loot filter menu so the filter is ready without manual XML editing.

steps.title: Set Early-Game Priorities
steps.description: During leveling, the filter should show health, resistances, movement speed, your main damage affixes, and useful class idols. At this stage, the goal is not perfection; it is making obvious upgrades and shard targets easy to spot.

steps.title: Tighten the Filter for Monolith and Dungeons
steps.description: Once Monolith farming starts, the filter should become stricter. Focus on wanted bases, high-value Exalted affixes, LP Uniques, class idols, crafting shard targets, and the boss or timeline drops your build is actually chasing.

steps.title: Separate Leveling Rules from Endgame Rules
steps.description: A filter that works in the campaign quickly becomes too loose in Empowered Monoliths. Many players swap into a dedicated endgame filter once Exalted drops become common so the screen does not flood with low-value rares.

steps.title: Use the New Season 4 Filter Tools
steps.description: Season 4 raised the rule cap to 200 and added separate customization for map icons and item beams, rule-number display options, and several new loot filter sounds. These upgrades make it much easier to build precise filters for modern endgame farming.

quickTips: Start with a community filter from Last Epoch Tools and customize it for your build.
Update your filter every time you enter a new endgame phase to avoid screen clutter.
Use separate filter profiles for leveling and endgame to keep rules clean.
Season 4 raised the rule cap to 200, giving you much more filtering precision.

lastEpochBlessingsGuide.title: Last Epoch Blessings Guide
lastEpochBlessingsGuide.subtitle: Pick the Right Blessings for Damage, Defense, and Farming
lastEpochBlessingsGuide.intro: Blessings are permanent Monolith rewards that shape both survivability and farming efficiency. Once you enter empowered timelines, Grand Blessings become a major part of endgame optimization. This module highlights the Blessings players most often prioritize when moving from basic Monolith progression into empowered farming.

items.why: One of the cleanest raw damage blessings for endgame crit setups.

items.bestFor: Mana-hungry Mage, Runemaster, and Spellblade builds
items.why: A strong quality-of-life blessing when your rotation or defensive loop depends on a deeper mana pool.

items.bestFor: Early empowered progression and resistance fixing
items.why: An easy way to free up suffix pressure on gear while stabilizing a build for harder content.

items.effect: +50% to 70% Critical Strike Avoidance and +51 to 90 Dodge Rating
items.bestFor: General survivability and early corruption pushing
items.why: A popular defensive blessing because it patches two common survival problems at once.

items.effect: +25% to 50% Chance to apply Shred Cold Resistance on Hit
items.why: A highly efficient blessing for cold builds that want more boss damage without changing gear.
A straightforward defensive option that stays useful across many classes and gearing stages.

items.effect: 16% to 22% Increased Unique Item Drop Rate
items.why: One of the clearest farming blessings when your next upgrade is a specific unique item.

lastEpochFactionsGuide.title: Last Epoch Factions Guide
lastEpochFactionsGuide.subtitle: Merchant's Guild or Circle of Fortune?
lastEpochFactionsGuide.intro: Factions decide how your character acquires gear in the endgame. This choice affects trading access, self-found efficiency, progression rhythm, and which item loop feels best for your build. Both factions use ranks, reputation, and Favor, but they reward very different playstyles.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: Trade-focused faction built around the Bazaar and player listings.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: Self-found faction built around loot quality, prophecies, and passive item-hunt bonuses.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: Buy and sell gear through the Bazaar using Favor alongside gold-based transactions.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: Spend Favor at the Observatory to obtain Prophecies, then use Lenses to shape what you see.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: 12 ranks total; gain reputation through normal gameplay and trading activity.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: 12 ranks total; gain reputation through normal gameplay and prophecy completion.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: Players who want faster build completion, targeted purchases, and market-driven gearing.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: Players who prefer farming their own gear, adapting to drops, and maximizing self-found value.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: Items purchased through trade require Merchant's Guild rank to equip, and bought items cannot be re-traded.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: Items found while aligned with Circle of Fortune require Circle of Fortune rank to equip.

comparisons.merchantsGuild: You can swap factions, but it functions more like a respec than a casual toggle. Reputation is shared account-wide, resets each Cycle, and carries into the permanent environment after a Cycle ends.
comparisons.circleOfFortune: You can swap factions, but it functions more like a respec than a casual toggle. Reputation is shared account-wide, resets each Cycle, and carries into the permanent environment after a Cycle ends.

lastEpochDungeonsGuide.title: Last Epoch Dungeons Guide
lastEpochDungeonsGuide.subtitle: Three Dungeons, Three Different Reward Loops
lastEpochDungeonsGuide.intro: Last Epoch dungeons are repeatable endgame activities with keys, tier scaling, and unique mechanics. Each one rewards a different kind of progression, from gold conversion to targeted gambling to legendary crafting.
lastEpochDungeonsGuide.rulesSummary: All dungeons are key-gated, repeatable endgame content with four tiers and daily modifiers. From Tier 2 onward, runs gain extra modifiers and better rewards.
rules: Each dungeon requires a specific key that is consumed on entry.
There are 4 tiers, and higher tiers unlock after clearing lower ones.
From Tier 2 onward, you receive additional modifiers and stronger rewards.
Dungeon Key Charms can modify runs, including a Portal Charm that skips directly to the boss.

dungeons.name: Lightless Arbor
dungeons.summary: A poison-themed dungeon built around darkness, navigation, and gold conversion at the end of the run.
dungeons.location: Ruined Era, near The Surface Waypoint

dungeons.name: Soulfire Bastion
dungeons.summary: A fire-and-necrotic dungeon where shield management and soul spending define the run.
dungeons.location: Imperial Era, near The Felled Wood Waypoint

dungeons.name: Temporal Sanctum
dungeons.summary: A high-value dungeon with era swapping, dangerous boss pressure, and direct access to legendary crafting.
dungeons.location: Ruined Era, near The Ruined Coast Waypoint

lastEpochUniqueItemsGuide.title: Last Epoch Unique and Legendary Items Guide
lastEpochUniqueItemsGuide.subtitle: How Uniques Become Long-Term Endgame Gear
lastEpochUniqueItemsGuide.intro: In Last Epoch, uniques are not just collectible items. They are the foundation for several late-game gear systems, especially Legendary crafting through Temporal Sanctum. This module explains the item systems players repeatedly search once they move beyond simple upgrades and start chasing build-defining gear.

systems.whatItIs: Items with fixed, build-defining modifiers that can grant effects not normally found on rare or exalted gear.
systems.coreRule: They are often the starting point for endgame chase gear and many builds revolve around one or more key uniques.
systems.connection: Target-farmed from bosses, timelines, dungeons, and broader loot systems.
systems.whySearch: Players usually look these up when deciding whether a build needs a specific item or when planning a gear transition.

systems.whatItIs: A property on unique items that ranges from 0 to 4.
systems.coreRule: Legendary Potential determines how many affixes from an exalted item can be transferred when creating a Legendary item.
systems.connection: The stronger the base unique, the more valuable even 1 LP can become.
systems.whySearch: LP is the main reason players care about whether a unique is just usable or truly endgame-worthy.

systems.system: Legendary Crafting in the Eternity Cache
systems.whatItIs: A crafting system unlocked after beating Chronomancer Julra in Temporal Sanctum.
systems.coreRule: You combine a unique with Legendary Potential and an exalted item of the same kind to create a Legendary item. At Tier 2 and above, the Eternity Cache lets you guarantee one affix.
systems.connection: Temporal Sanctum is the practical bridge between unique drops and long-term endgame gear.
systems.whySearch: This is the core upgrade path for turning a good unique into a real chase item.

systems.whatItIs: A special unique-item system that uses Weaver's Will instead of normal Legendary Potential.
systems.coreRule: Weaver uniques drop with 5 to 28 Weaver's Will and gain affixes or upgrade affix tiers while enemies are killed nearby, including upgrades up to Tier 7.
systems.connection: It creates a separate progression path where the item evolves with use instead of being slammed in the Eternity Cache.
systems.whySearch: Players search this when they want to know whether a Weaver item is worth leveling up rather than replacing.

systems.whatItIs: A later-game class of extremely powerful unique items.
systems.coreRule: You can equip only one Primordial item of any kind at a time.
systems.connection: These compete directly with other endgame slot decisions because of their single-item power budget.
systems.whySearch: They matter when players are min-maxing final gear and deciding which single high-impact slot to dedicate.

lastEpochIdolsGuide.title: Last Epoch Idols Guide
lastEpochIdolsGuide.intro: Idols are a unique equipment system in Last Epoch. They provide powerful passive bonuses and come in different shapes that fit into your idol inventory grid. Class-specific idol affixes make them a major source of build power.

cards.name: Idol Slot Unlocks
cards.description: Idol slots are unlocked through campaign side quests. Completing all idol quests opens the full grid for maximum passive power.

cards.name: Idol Shapes and Sizes
cards.description: Idols come in different rectangular shapes. Fitting them efficiently into your grid maximizes the number of active bonuses.

cards.name: Class-Specific Affixes
cards.description: Each class has access to unique idol affixes that can significantly boost specific skills and playstyles.

lastEpochWeaverTreeGuide.title: Last Epoch Weaver Tree Guide
lastEpochWeaverTreeGuide.intro: The Weaver Tree is a seasonal progression system that adds account-wide bonuses through node selection. Early pathing decisions between XP and loot nodes shape your entire seasonal progression.

cards.name: Early Node Pathing
cards.description: Prioritize XP nodes early in the season to accelerate character leveling. Switch to loot nodes once your build stabilizes.

cards.name: XP vs Loot Choices
cards.description: XP nodes provide faster leveling while loot nodes improve drop quality. The optimal path depends on your seasonal goals.

cards.name: Seasonal Planning
cards.description: Plan your Weaver Tree path before committing points. The tree changes how Monolith farming and progression feel throughout the season.

lastEpochBossGuide.title: Last Epoch Boss Guide
lastEpochBossGuide.intro: Last Epoch bosses test your build's defensive stats, damage uptime, and mechanical awareness. Campaign bosses introduce core mechanics while Monolith and Dungeon bosses demand refined builds.

cards.name: Campaign Bosses
cards.description: Story bosses like Lagon test positioning and dodge skills. Focus on capping elemental resistances and maintaining health sustain.

cards.name: Monolith Bosses
cards.description: Timeline bosses guard blessings and have fixed mechanics. Learning their patterns is essential for efficient blessing farming.

cards.name: Dungeon Bosses
cards.description: Julra, Mountain Beneath, and Cremorus each have unique fight mechanics tied to their dungeon's core system.

cards.name: Defensive Stat Checks
cards.description: Most boss fights require capped resistances, adequate health pool, and at least one form of health sustain to survive reliably.

lastEpochRespecGuide.title: Last Epoch Respec Guide
lastEpochRespecGuide.intro: Last Epoch allows flexible respeccing of passives and skills, with recent updates also enabling mastery respec. Understanding when and how to respec saves gold and avoids unnecessary character rerolls.

cards.name: Passive Respec
cards.description: Remove passive points one at a time for a small gold cost. The cost scales with character level but remains affordable.

cards.name: Skill Respec
cards.description: Despecialize a skill to free its slot. The new skill starts at specialization level 1 and needs to be leveled through combat.

cards.name: Mastery Respec
cards.description: Recent updates allow mastery changes. This is a significant decision that resets mastery-specific passives and skills.

cards.name: When to Respec vs Reroll
cards.description: Respec when adjusting within the same build archetype. Reroll a new character when you want a completely different class or playstyle.

faq.title: Last Epoch
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Last Epoch builds, classes, and endgame

questions.question: What is Last Epoch?
questions.answer: Last Epoch is a time-travel action RPG developed by Eleventh Hour Games. It features 5 base classes with 15 masteries, deep crafting, loot filters, and endgame systems including Monolith of Fate, Dungeons, and Arena.

questions.question: How many classes does Last Epoch have?
questions.answer: Last Epoch has 5 base classes: Sentinel, Mage, Acolyte, Primalist, and Rogue. Each base class branches into 3 masteries for a total of 15 unique subclasses.

questions.question: What is the best starter class in Last Epoch?
questions.answer: Sentinel into Void Knight is widely considered the safest first-character path in Last Epoch. Paladin and Sorcerer are also strong beginner-friendly choices with forgiving mechanics.

questions.question: How does crafting work in Last Epoch?
questions.answer: Last Epoch crafting uses shards to add or upgrade affixes on equipment. Each craft consumes Forging Potential. Glyphs and Runes provide additional crafting options, and the Temporal Sanctum dungeon enables legendary crafting.

questions.question: What is the Last Epoch endgame?
questions.answer: Last Epoch endgame centers on Monolith of Fate, where you push corruption and earn blessings. Additional endgame content includes Dungeons with unique mechanics, Arena for wave survival, and faction-based gearing through Merchant's Guild or Circle of Fortune.

questions.question: Can you respec in Last Epoch?
questions.answer: Yes. Last Epoch allows passive and skill respeccing at any time for a small gold cost. Recent updates also added mastery respec, allowing players to change their subclass.

questions.question: Is Last Epoch multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Last Epoch supports online co-op for up to 4 players. You can also play solo offline or in online solo mode with access to shared stash and trading.

questions.question: What platforms is Last Epoch available on?
questions.answer: Last Epoch is available on PC through Steam. The game launched in full release on February 21, 2024, after an extended Early Access period.

factions.whatIsDescription: Last Epoch has two item factions that change how you acquire gear. Merchant's Guild enables trading while Circle of Fortune boosts drop rates and adds prophecy farming.

whySteps.step2Description: Higher ranks unlock more powerful faction features and better rewards
whySteps.step3Description: Switching factions resets all progress, so understanding both options prevents wasted time
